Tines 

What do you think? …

Abdullah Javaid [...] Tines 

What do you think? …

Abdullah Javaid

Fun idea and it loads fast. Watching T3 Chat sit at number 1 makes the ranking feel instantly believable, and the YC batch filter is a smart touch for checking who is loud in your batch. Spent about ten minutes poking around before commenting.

One thing that tripped me: the search box reads like I can look up any X account, but it only matches accounts already on the board, and it seems to match website domains too. I typed vercel and got one random person whose portfolio is hosted on vercel.app instead of anything Vercel related. Matching handle and display name first would fix that. Also the 24H column is all dashes right now, might be worth hiding it until the data lands.

Upvoted, curious how big the board gets after today. [...] Upvoted, curious how big the board gets after today.

Varun Mishra

This is a fun way to spot who's consistently building in public instead of relying on follower counts alone. The weekly growers view sounds especially useful for discovering startups before they become obvious.

results.title: The Project Yapper Leaderboard shows which individual ...
results.content: @FogoXYZ @Humanityprot @HyperbolicFi @InfinexApp @InitiaProtocol @LombardFi @MapleStoryU @Mega\_ETH @MitosisNetwork @Monad\_xyz @MultipliFi @OpenLedger\_xyz @Paradex\_ @SkateXyz @Somnia\_Network @soonsyndicate @sophon\_xyz @succinctxyz @Theoriq\_xyz @ThriveProtocol @Union\_build -- My Alpha as a Yapper: Honestly, the best move is to catch a project before it blows up. Pick one that isn’t too talked about yet, but seems legit That’s how you dominate a leaderboard early and maybe even catch the team’s attention. But that said, Twitter is like an echo chamber, I always talked about Qubic AI and 0n1force and Cool cats before, so i am kinda still stuck in that bubble. If I talk about other things, I almost never get the engagement that much BUT after doing a week and actually exploring into [...] Yo, if you’re like me, a regular Kaito Yapper try make waves, you’ve probably seen the Project Yapper Leaderboards and wondered: “Am I getting points for this?” Here’s the simple breakdown: What it tracks: The Project Yapper Leaderboard shows which individual X (Twitter) accounts are helping push the mindshare (aka YAPS literally) for a specific project. So if you’re tweeting about one project a lot and getting engagement, you might show up there. But I found out if i just talk about cyrpo and NFTS, in general, I get em toooooo. -- Do you get Yap points for this and thisss??? Nah. That leaderboard and your Yap points are two different things. Yap points come from all your crypto tweets (not just about one project). The Project Leaderboard just shows who’s talking the most (or best) about [...] just shows who’s talking the most (or best) about a certain project. Only individuals are counted, yeah, sorry @aixbt\_agent lmaooo.. Only real people, not brand accounts, show up. Kaito’s AI checks your profile pic, bio, tweets, etc., to see if you’re an individual or a company, so those AI agents have a seperate tab -- Projects You Should Start Yapping About (Especially Before TGE) Here’s where I add my personal strategy. Now’s the time to build mindshare early. You don’t need to wait till everyone’s already in. You can mention the name, the token, or even just the handle all good for boosting your leaderboard presence. Pre-TGE Tokens and Blockchains Projects to Watch: @0Gnetwork @AlloraNetwork @CampNetwork @defiapp @EclipseFND @FogoXYZ @Humanityprot @HyperbolicFi @InfinexApp

results.title: Announcing the dYdX Yapper Leaderboard
results.content:
This is dYdX’s first major SocialFi activation, a program designed to reward culture carriers, surface emerging voices, and amplify the stories that move the ecosystem forward.

→ View the live leaderboard

## What is the Yapper Leaderboard?

The Yapper Leaderboard is a real-time scoreboard that highlights which accounts are contributing the most Mindshare to the dYdX ecosystem.

Mindshare measures the share of voice an individual account brings to a project. It’s not about shouting the loudest or tweeting the most, it’s about the quality of the content, the focus of the commentary, and the authenticity of the engagement.

Using Kaito’s intelligence engine, the leaderboard rewards: [...] ## Leaving site [...] in connection with the use of this content. By continuing to access this content, you agree to the above and accept the possibility of changes in the information provided.
